benchmark_notes.selected_for: Synthesis benchmark extracted from existing question files

questions.question_text: Longest increasing path in matrix (from leetcode.com) Given an m x n integers matrix, develop an efficient algorithm to find the length of the longest increasing path in a matrix. From each cell, you can either move in four directions: left, right, up, or down. You may not move diagonally or move outside the boundary (i.e., wrap-around is not allowed).
Let Sigma={a,b}, design a context-free grammar for the language generated by the regular expression b*(abb*)*
Design an unambiguous context-free grammar for the language L={a^i b^j | j <= i <= 2j}. Note: you still get a small partial credit if your grammar is ambiguous
Prove that the Knapsack problem (as defined in the lecture) is in NP.

A Hamiltonian path in an undirected or directed graph is a path that visits each vertex exactly once. A Tonian path in an undirected or directed graph is a path that visits at least half of the vertices in the graph. Let HAMILTONIAN and TONIAN be the problems of determining whether a graph has a Hamiltonian path and a Tonian path, respectively. Prove that HAMILTONIAN ≤p TONIAN, meaning that any instance of the HAMILTONIAN problem can be reduced in polynomial time to an instance of the TONIAN problem.
